title: Black Box Theatre Configuration
openingLine: I'm trying to outfit our black box for the next school year. We do everything in there — straight plays, devised work, dance, sometimes a small ensemble. I keep getting pitched riser systems that are basically the same thing. What makes yours different?
customerContext: You're the TD for a magnet performing arts school. You have a dedicated $185K from the capital plan for black box improvements. You've used Wenger acoustic shells in the main hall and liked them, but you've never bought platforms or seating from them. You're skeptical of reps who lead with product before understanding the program.

hiddenInfo.reveal: $185K from the capital plan, locked in for this fiscal year.
Need install complete by August 1 — we open the season the third week of September.
Plays, devised work, dance, small ensembles, occasional all-school assemblies. Configurations change weekly.
Resetting the room takes 4 students 90 minutes. Killing rehearsal time.
Me, the principal, and the district facilities lead. Principal trusts my recommendation.
We have Diva acoustic shells in the main hall. Bought them four years ago. Happy with them.
Also looking at audience seating wagons and a portable lighting grid solution.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Asked about the variety of uses for the space
Asked about reset time / current pain point
Asked for current room dimensions and any drawings
Asked about prior Wenger product experience

Solution Framing.behavior: Differentiated based on flexibility and reset speed, not just product specs
Offered to demo the product or arrange a site visit to a peer school

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Probed for adjacent needs (seating, lighting grid, storage)
Asked about other spaces in the building that could benefit

BANT.behavior: Identified principal and district facilities as decision makers

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Mapped lead times against the August 1 install date
Set follow-up tasks with specific dates

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Provided literature or reference school visits
